_comment: Coverage ratchet baseline. CI fails if any metric below this drops on a PR. Refresh by running `node tools/check-coverage-baseline.mjs --update` after intentionally adjusting tests.

crap._comment: CRAP = ccn² × (1 − cov)² + ccn, per file. The CRAP score floors at ccn (perfect coverage gives CRAP == ccn), so a file with intrinsic ccn > 30 stays in the high-risk bucket no matter how thoroughly tested it is. max_le and p95_le are the meaningful gates; high_risk_le tracks codebase growth (new feature files with ccn > 30 add to the count) and gets refreshed when the count drifts up. Bumped from 17 → 20 in 2026-05 (new aniskip / availability / anilist / play_select / play_cache files) and 20 → 21 (proptest functions in play_select pushed the file's intrinsic ccn over 30 — the proptests add real value, accept the bookkeeping tick).
